pattern: A monotonic stack of (price, span) groups collapses every earlier day whose price did not exceed today's into a single span, so each day's answer is found in amortized constant time.
hints: The span for today counts a run of recent days, so it depends only on how far back prices stayed at or below today's price.
When a new price arrives, every immediately preceding day with a price at or below it is already absorbed into a longer run and never needs to be examined again.
Keep a stack of (price, span) pairs: start today's span at one, then while the top price is at or below today's, pop it and add its span to today's; push today's price with the accumulated span.

edge_inputs.description: mixed prices producing growing spans on later peaks
strictly increasing prices give spans counting all prior days
strictly decreasing prices keep every span at one
equal prices count toward the span since the test is at-or-below
a tall peak after a valley swallows the entire history

stress.description: long stream of prices over a narrow band to force many stack collapses and ties
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['StockSpanner']
    args = [[]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        ops.append('next')
        args.append([rng.randint(1, 40)])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: narrow value range maximises ties and long collapsing runs
